quiz.question:
Black takes on d5. 

It seems Black is an exchange up, and they also have a pawn. 

With perfect play, what should the result of this game be?

feedback.text:
Exactly. 

The pawns could be traded, and Rook vs Knight isn't winning for any of the parties involved.

distractors.text: Black is winning because they're an exchange up, the pawns are irrelevant.

feedback.text: A simple exchange isn't enough to win.

distractors.text: Black is winning because they have a rook and a pawn. Black only has to win the g-pawn and this would be winning.

feedback.text: Sure, if Black wins the g-pawn, they win. But White can easily defend with his king and knight.

distractors.text: Black is lost. The knight overpowers the rook.

feedback.text: That isn't the case. The rook is generally stronger than the knight.
